questions.question_text: ParsedQuestion: The difference in simple interest on a sum of money invested at 4% per annum for five years and for three years is ₹784. Find the simple interest (in ₹) on the same sum invested at the same rate of interest for eight years.

ParsedQuestion: ABCD is a quadrilateral in which AB || DC, and E and F are the mid points of the diagonals AC and BD, respectively. If AB = 60 cm, BC = 65 cm, DC = 18 cm, and AD = 10 cm, then what is the length (in cm) of EF?
ParsedQuestion: Simplify: $9^2 + \sqrt{4^2} - 7\sqrt{16} - 7$

ParsedQuestion: A, B, and C can complete a work in 10, 12, and 15 days, respectively. A and B work together for 3 days, after which A withdraws. B and C then work together. Find the number of days required by B and C to complete the remaining work.

ParsedQuestion: A hollow spherical shell is made of a metal of density $39 \text{ g/cm}^3$. Its internal and external radii are $12\text{ cm}$ and $13\text{ cm}$, respectively. What is the weight (in kg) of the shell? $$\left( \text{Use } \pi = \frac{22}{7} \text{ and Density} = \frac{\text{Mass}}{\text{Volume}} \right)$$

ParsedQuestion: A train passes two persons walking in the same direction in which the train is going. These persons are walking at the rates of $2\text{ km/hr}$ and $4\text{ km/hr}$, respectively, and are completely crossed by the train in 9 seconds and 10 seconds, respectively. What is the length of the train?
ParsedQuestion: The fourth proportional to the first number, the second number, and 20 is 36. If the sum of the first two numbers is 14, find the geometric mean of the first two numbers.
ParsedQuestion: The sum of two positive numbers is 67 and their product is 1012. The positive difference between them is:

ParsedQuestion: In a class, there are 10 boys more than girls. Each girl has 3 pencils and each boy has 2 pencils with them. The total number of pencils in the class is 170. The ratio of girls to boys in the class is _______.
ParsedQuestion: A and B can do a piece of work in 12 days and 15 days, respectively. Both work for 4 days and then A leaves. How long (in days) will B take to complete the remaining work?
ParsedQuestion: Which of the following numbers is divisible by 11?
ParsedQuestion: Let both a and b be prime numbers such that $(a^2 + b)$ is also a prime number less than 20. What is the number of possible values of b?
ParsedQuestion: What is the mode of the following data?\n\n 40, 47, 54, 45, 50, 43, 53, 52, 53, 49, 40, 50, 42, 52, 44, 52, 47, 55
ParsedQuestion: What is the greatest length of a measuring rod that can exactly measure three fabric pieces of lengths 7 m 35 cm, 9 m 45 cm, and 10 m 50 cm?

ParsedQuestion: A solid metallic sphere is melted and recast into a cone whose radius is equal to the radius of the sphere. If the height of the cone is 84 cm, what was the volume of the original sphere? $$\left( \text{Use } \pi = \frac{22}{7} \right)$$.
ParsedQuestion: If $p$ is the third proportional to 6 and 18, and $q$ is the third proportional to $p$ and 54, find the value of $q$.
ParsedQuestion: The marked price of a refrigerator is ₹4,884. The shopkeeper allows a discount of 10% and gains 8%. If no discount is allowed, his gain percentage would be:

ParsedQuestion: The following ratios are given:
 $a : b = 2 : 3$
 $b : c = 4 : 5$
 $c : d = 3 : 2$
 $d : e = 5 : 4$
 What is the value of the ratio $a : e$?
ParsedQuestion: If a trader purchases 50 notebooks for ₹1000 and sells all at a gain of 20%, what is the selling price of each notebook?
ParsedQuestion: A marker costs ₹43. A store offers "Buy 5, Get 2 Free". What is the effective price per marker? [Give your answer in ₹, correct to two places after decimal.]
ParsedQuestion: The sum of the present ages of a father and his son is 50 years. Five years ago, the father's age was three times the son's age. By how many years is the father older than his son?
ParsedQuestion: A scooter travels 40 km at 20 km/hr and returns 60 km at the same speed. What is the average speed?
ParsedQuestion: A tech firm's revenue decreased from ₹2,50,000 to ₹2,12,500. What is the percentage decrease in the revenue?

ParsedQuestion: The LCM of the numbers 5, 3.2 and 0.03 is:
ParsedQuestion: If the income tax is increased by 18%, the net income is reduced by 2%. Find the original rate of income tax.

ParsedQuestion: An article is sold at a profit of 58%. If the cost price is increased by ₹20 and the selling price is reduced by ₹74, then the profit would be 52.5%. What is the original cost price (in ₹) of the article?
ParsedQuestion: What sum (in ₹) will earn a simple interest of ₹1,440 in 3 years at 8% per year rate of interest?

ParsedQuestion: Simplify: $$\left\{ \frac{20}{33} \div \frac{20}{7} \right\} \div \left( \frac{3}{5} \times \frac{10}{9} + \frac{2}{11} \right) + \frac{7}{4} \div \frac{28}{23} \text{ of } \frac{23}{4}$$
ParsedQuestion: Solar energy is globally measured in the unit of KWh. The physical quantity corresponding to the unit KWh is __________.

options.text: Appointment of state governors by the Centre

 (i) ROM is faster than RAM because it stores permanent system instructions accessed less frequently by the CPU.

ParsedQuestion: Refer to the given number series and answer the question that follows. All numbers are single-digit numbers only. Counting to be done from left to right only.

 (Left) 9 6 3 1 8 7 2 9 5 6 1 4 7 5 3 4 5 6 2 6 8 7 2 3 7 6 5 8 (Right)

 How many such even digits are there which are immediately preceded by a perfect square and immediately followed by an odd digit?
 (NOTE: 1 is also a perfect square.)

ParsedQuestion: Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four letter-cluster pairs are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which is the one that does not belong to that group? (Note: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter cluster.)

ParsedQuestion: In a certain code language,
 A $ B means 'A is the father of B',
 A @ B means 'A is the sister of B',
 A # B means 'A is the wife of B' and
 A & B means 'A is the brother of B'.

 How is L related to T if 'L # I $ G @ H & T'?

ParsedQuestion: What should come in place of the '?' in the given series?\n\n 8 6 9 23 87 ?

ParsedQuestion: Av, Bg, Ck, Dq, Et, Fx and Gp are sitting around a circular table, facing the centre.\n Bg sits second to the right of Av. Gp sits second to the left of Av. Dq sits third to the right of Gp. Ck sits to the immediate right of Fx.\n How many people sit between Et and Ck when counted from the left of Et?
ParsedQuestion: What should come in place of '?' in the given series based on the English alphabetical order?\n\n URW GDI SPU EBG ?

ParsedQuestion: Seven boxes, B, C, D, E, Q, R and S, are kept one over the other, but not necessarily in the same order. Only one box is kept above E. Only two boxes are kept between C and E. B is kept immediately above E. Only Q is kept immediately below S. D is kept at some place below B and at some place above R. How many boxes are kept between B and S?
ParsedQuestion: MJ 8 is related to OM -6 in a certain way. In the same way, RW 12 is related to TZ -2. To which of the following is UR 1 related, following the same logic?

ParsedQuestion: Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four letter-cluster pairs are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which is the one that does not belong to that group?\n (Note: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter-cluster pairs.)
ParsedQuestion: In a certain code language, ‘DOLT’ is coded as ‘4963’ and ‘LOTS’ is coded as ‘6435’. What is the code for ‘S’ in the given code language?
ParsedQuestion: Select the pair which follows the same pattern as that followed by the two sets of pairs given below. Both pairs follow the same pattern.\n QVI : KZJ\n USH : OWI
ParsedQuestion: What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series based on the English alphabetical order?\n\n FHD GIE HJF IKG ?

ParsedQuestion: Seven people H, I, J, K, W, Y and Z are sitting around a circular table facing the center of the table. Only one person sits between H and W when counted from the left of W. Z sits third to the right of I. J sits third to the left of K. Z sits to the immediate right of J. Y is not an immediate neighbor of J. How many people sit between Y and W when counted from the right of Y?

ParsedQuestion: If ‘A’ stands for ‘$\div$’, ‘B’ stands for ‘$\times$’, ‘C’ stands for ‘$+$’ and ‘D’ stands for ‘$-$’, what will come in place of the question mark ‘?’ in the following equation?\n 81 A 3 D 7 B 2 C 16 = ?
ParsedQuestion: Which of the following letter-number clusters will replace the question mark (?) in the given series to make it logically complete?\n\n GAO 81 , EYM 75 , CWK 69 , AUI 63 , ?

ParsedQuestion: If ‘A’ stands for ‘$+$’, ‘B’ stands for ‘$-$’, ‘C’ stands for ‘$\times$’, and ‘D’ stands for ‘$\div$’, then what will come in place of the question mark (?) in the following equation?\n\n 25 A 51 D 3 B 6 C 5 = ?

ParsedQuestion: In a certain code language,
 A % B means ‘A is the sister of B’
 A € B means ‘A is the son of B’
 A = B means ‘A is the father of B’
 A ? B means ‘A is the daughter of B’
 Based on the above, how is B related to X if 'B % C ? O € P = X'?

ParsedQuestion: Read the given statement(s) and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statement(s).

 Statements:
 All tigers are chairs.
 All chairs are monks.

 Conclusions:
 (I) Some monks are chairs.
 (II) All tigers are monks.

ParsedQuestion: Refer to the following series and answer the question. (Counting to be done from left to right only.) (All numbers are single-digit numbers only.)
 (Left) 3 5 8 9 7 4 1 5 9 6 2 3 6 5 8 7 4 1 5 9 8 6 3 2 1 5 4 7 8 4 1 2 5 9 8 7 4 1 5 6 2 3 9 8 7 8 9 7 5 1 (Right)

 How many such odd digits are there, each of which is immediately preceded by a perfect cube and immediately followed by an even digit? (NOTE: 1 is also a perfect cube.)

ParsedQuestion: Refer to the following series and answer the question. (Counting to be done from left to right only.) (All numbers are single-digit numbers only.)
 (Left) 7 5 9 2 7 9 4 1 5 1 7 8 3 2 8 4 1 2 3 8 2 7 9 (Right)

 How many such odd digits are there, each of which is immediately preceded by an odd digit and also immediately followed by an even digit?
ParsedQuestion: FR 6 is related to KN -6 in a certain way. In the same way, LU 9 is related to QQ -3. To which of the following is LS 14 related, following the same logic?

ParsedQuestion: Mr. Vasu starts from Point A and drives 23 km towards the south. He then takes a left turn, drives 19 km, turns left and drives 36 km. He then takes a left turn and drives 19 km, turns left drives 9 km. He then takes a right turn and drives 5 km. He takes a final left turn, drives 4 km and stops at Point P. How far (shortest distance) and towards which direction should he drive in order to reach Point A again? (All turns are 90-degree turns only unless specified.)
ParsedQuestion: In a certain code language, ‘VIBE’ is coded as ‘4395’ and ‘BITE’ is coded as ‘5914’. What is the code for ‘T’ in the given code language?

ParsedQuestion: Select the set in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following sets.
 (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into their constituent digits. E.g. 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding/subtracting/multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.)

 (14, 7, 21)
 (56, 28, 42)

ParsedQuestion: In a row of 15 students facing the north, Yusuf is $10^{\text{th}}$ from the right end. If Suman is $4^{\text{th}}$ to the left of Yusuf, what is Suman's position from the left end of the line?

ParsedQuestion: Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four letter-cluster pairs are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which is the one that does not belong to that group?\n (Note: The odd man out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter cluster.)
ParsedQuestion: Which two numbers should be interchanged to make the given equation correct?\n\n $(28 \div 4) \times 6 + 14 \times 3 - 7 \times 9 + (66 \div 2) + 38 = 67$

ParsedQuestion: Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements.

 Statements: Some poets are writers. All writers are dreamers.
 Conclusions:
 (I) Some dreamers are poets.
 (II) All poets are dreamers.
